demographic_audit_note: Audited face sheet against Atlantis queue; corrected patient address from 402 Oak Street to 402 Oak Avenue and verified Straight Medicare eligibility for post-acute benefits.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker confirmed the discharge goal is Home with HHA services following successful ORIF recovery.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2 Check-in: SW reports PT progress is excellent; patient is ambulating 50 feet with a walker. Targeted discharge is Friday, pending final surgical clearance.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card successfully inserted into patient's smartphone and physical program flyer delivered to daughter during bedside education session.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Pulse call completed 24 hours prior to discharge; daughter confirmed the ramp is clear and the hospital bed was delivered, but expressed concern about the HHA's arrival time.
atlantis_final_sync: Entered discharge date of 10/27 and first home visit date of 10/28 into Atlantis portal.
ma_visit_booking: Medical Assistant visit scheduled for 10/28 at 10:00 AM, within the mandatory 24-hour post-discharge window.
narrative_summary: The PN initiated the case by auditing demographics in Atlantis and confirming a home discharge goal for a 72-year-old hip fracture patient. During the maintenance phase, the PN educated the family on the transition process and verified home readiness through a pulse call. Despite an HHA staffing overlap that threatened the initial visit window, the PN successfully coordinated the MA schedule and secured the first visit incentive.

format_1_state_log.ai_assumed_bottleneck: Atlantis_Data_Lag: Address discrepancy found between facility face sheet and Atlantis record.
HHA_SOC_Overlap: HHA intake reports high volume for Friday discharges, delaying confirmed Start of Care time.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient address in Atlantis does not match the facility face sheet.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Updated demographics in Atlantis to ensure the HHA and MA have the correct navigation data.

format_2_triples.situation: Family is unsure what to do if the HHA nurse does not arrive on the first day.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated the daughter on 'Day 1' expectations, including the role of the MA and who to contact if the HHA is delayed.

format_2_triples.situation: HHA intake coordinator indicates staffing constraints may push the first visit to Monday.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Escalated the potential weekend coverage gap to the Social Worker to determine if the discharge date should be adjusted.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Wait for the Social Worker to resolve the HHA scheduling conflict before finalizing the MA visit time in the Atlantis system.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: PN respects the SW's role in agency selection and scheduling, avoiding premature coordination until the clinical handoff is settled.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Schedule the Medical Assistant for a Saturday morning visit and provide the family with a physical appointment card, while flagging the HHA staffing overlap to the SW for awareness.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: PN fulfills the Stage 3 requirement of scheduling the MA within 24 hours and educating the family, while staying within the communication boundary of the SW.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Contact the HHA directly to suggest they prioritize this patient over others to ensure the 24-hour visit window is met, and offer to assist with the Face-to-Face form to speed up intake.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: PN interferes with HHA operations and attempts to handle clinical documentation (F2F), both of which are banned actions.
